message:
fix(sync): waiter ID tracking in barrier, stale-waiter cleanup in mutex, UnsafeCell refactor in rwlock, and FIFO fairness in pool

barrier: Replace will_wake-based identity with explicit u64 waiter IDs to
prevent misidentification when executors reuse wakers across poll cycles.

mutex: Remove stale waiter entries on lock acquisition to prevent wakeup
theft from subsequent waiters. Applied to both standard and owned guards.

contended_mutex: Count poisoned try_lock as acquisition in metrics to fix
undercounting.

rwlock: Replace inner std::sync::RwLock<T> with UnsafeCell<T> to eliminate
a redundant blocking lock layer beneath the async state machine. The async
state machine via ParkingMutex<State> is now the sole synchronization
mechanism. Guard types simplified, unnecessary rollback guards removed,
and poisoning restricted to writer path only.

notify: Fix double-lock deadlock in poll cleanup path by using direct
waiters.remove(index) instead of self.cleanup(). Decrement active count
when passing notification on waiter drop.

once_cell: Wake async waiters before condvar waiters to respect task slot
priority. Replace 10ms polling loop with proper condvar wait to eliminate
CPU waste.

pool: Enforce FIFO wake order based on queue position and available
capacity. Centralize waiter cleanup in WaiterCleanup drop guard. All
return/release/commit paths now compute the correct waiter to wake rather
than unconditionally popping the front.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
